_note: Casper's brain — owner-editable. Each intent: id (slug), keywords (lowercase trigger words/phrases; multi-word phrases match as substrings and score higher), answer (Casper's reply, short, in your voice — keep the *action* asterisks for cat gestures), action (null = speak only; {type:'rec',cat:'anime|manga|movies|books|shows'} = light that lantern + reveal titles; {type:'chef'} = chef's choice card), followups (chip labels shown after the answer). Edit freely — the matcher reads this verbatim, nothing is hardcoded in JS. config.threshold tunes how sure a match must be before Casper answers vs. falls back.

config.greeting: *Casper blinks slowly* …you came to the counter. Ask me about the menu: anime, manga, films, books, shows. Or about the human who runs this cart.
config.fallback: *tilts head* I didn't quite catch that. Try one of these:

intents.answer: *sits up* Evening. The lanterns are lit and the kettle's on. Ask about the menu, or about the human who runs the cart.
*ears perk up* Oh, a name? He'd love that. Type your name (and a LinkedIn or where you're from, if you like) and I'll pass it along. Totally optional.
*stretches* I take two kinds of orders. Recommendations: anime, manga, films, books, shows. And questions about Abhuday: his work, skills, wins, how to reach him. Pick one ↓
*sits up* This cart belongs to Kunwar Abhuday Singh, aka ZaGrayWolf. A software engineer working where ML, robotics, and DevOps overlap. CS undergrad at Manipal. He feeds me; I run the counter.

*counts on a paw* Right now: Agentic OS Platform Engineering at BookMyShow this summer, plus ongoing deep-learning research with KLIV at IIT Kharagpur and robotics with Team RoboManipal. Last summer it was AI agents at Incubyte.
*nods at the menu board* BookMyShow (Agentic OS Platform), Incubyte (AI agents), KLIV / IIT Kharagpur (edge ML research), and Team RoboManipal (robotics). Ask about any one of them.

*ears perk* Platform engineering for the Agentic OS at BookMyShow. Pipelines and reliability at the scale where a million people want a ticket to the same show at the same second. The build: a platform that wires AI agents into company workflows so the repetitive ops run themselves.

*tail flicks* At Incubyte he shipped 20+ enterprise AI agents on Google Cloud with the Agno framework, wired into real corporate systems over RESTful microservices. Manual workload down 60%, throughput up 40%. In production, for real clients.

*perks up* KLIV Group at IIT Kharagpur, deep-learning research. He benchmarked 17 CNNs and Transformers across 8 input resolutions on 4 edge platforms, instrumented for latency, throughput, page faults, and activation memory. Results: 35% faster, 90%+ accuracy, 40% smaller. Not one lucky run, a full study. It has its own one-shot chapter at oneshot.html.

*sits tall* Team RoboManipal: real-time 6-DOF pose estimation in ROS + OpenCV at 20+ FPS. That stack went to RoboCup@Work: Top 8 in Germany, Top 10 in Brazil. The robot always knows exactly where it is.

*counts on a paw* Languages: python, c++, javascript, sql. ML: pytorch, tensorflow, scikit-learn, numpy, pandas. Infra: docker, google-cloud, kubernetes, linux, git. Robotics: ros / ros2, opencv, aruco, jetson, coral. Plus quant (black-scholes, monte-carlo) and agent work with agno.
*tilts head* B.Tech in Computer Science at Manipal Institute of Technology (MIT MAHE), 2023 to 2027, CGPA 8.09 / 10.

*tail flicks proudly* RoboCup@Work: Top 8 in Germany, Top 10 in Brazil, the only Indian team. ABU Robocon 2024: 99/100. IISc RoboNautica: runners-up. Aether BytesBattle: national winners. And a Python library live on PyPI.

*slides the menu over* The headliners: advance-conv-benchmarks (a published PyPI library), Custom ArUco 6-DOF tracking, a quant Option-Pricing engine, and an ensemble object detector at 98%+ accuracy. 30+ repos at github.com/ZaGrayWolf.
followups: tell me about the quant work

intents.answer: *taps the counter* His research at KLIV, IIT Kharagpur: 17 models, 8 resolutions, 4 edge devices, all quantized INT8. The whole study is drawn as a one-shot chapter, breakage and all. Find it at oneshot.html, or through the projects floor.
*proud blink* advance-conv-benchmarks: every convolution variant, counted and raced. FLOPs and parameters across PyTorch and TensorFlow, packaged so anyone can `pip install` it. It's live on PyPI.
followups: tell me about the quant work

intents.answer: *ears twitch* The Option-Pricing engine: Black-Scholes and Monte Carlo with the full Greeks, risk estimation, and a simulator that chews through thousands of paths with automated rebalancing.
*slides a card forward* Reach him at abhuday2656@gmail.com, github.com/ZaGrayWolf, or LinkedIn. The RESUME ↓ link up top has the one-page PDF. He's open to good problems.

*looks you dead in the eye* He ships things that have to work in the real world: agents in production, robots at international finals, a library people actually install. Not demos. Things that run, on deadlines, and either work or don't.
*glances out* Noida and Manipal, India. Works remote for most of it; travels for the robotics.
*purrs* I'm Casper. White, calm, behind the counter. There's a pixel version of me roaming the alley back home. That's my cousin. Pet him if you see him.
*nudges the ANIME lantern* It's lit. Here's the shelf. Pick a title and I'll slide it over.
*nudges the MANGA lantern* Lit. Take your pick from the shelf.
*nudges the MOVIES lantern* Lit. Here's what's on the reel.
*nudges the BOOKS lantern* Lit. The shelf's yours.
*nudges the SHOWS lantern* Lit. Pull up a stool, here's what to binge.
*rings a little bell* Chef's choice. Coming across the counter now.
